question_title: Will the community prediction be higher than 11.00% on 2026-04-09 for the Metaculus question "Will shipping traffic through the Strait of Hormuz return to normal before April 27, 2026?"?
summary: This meta-prediction asks whether the Metaculus CP for Strait of Hormuz shipping normalization will be above 11% on April 9. The CP is currently exactly at 11% (down from 20% a week ago), reflecting a strong downward trend. The underlying situation is dire: only 3-7 ships transit daily versus the 45 needed, with an active US-Israel-Iran war, Houthis joining the conflict, and Iran formalizing military authorization requirements for strait passage. However, active diplomatic talks (Pakistan quartet with Turkey, Egypt, Saudi Arabia) are specifically focused on reopening Hormuz, and Trump's April 6 deadline creates a potential forcing function. My scenario decomposition yields ~32-33%: the strong downward trend and worsening ground situation favor continued CP decline, but there's meaningful probability (~18-20%) of a ceasefire or diplomatic breakthrough that could spike the CP above the threshold.

factors.description: Strong downward CP trend: 20% → 11% in one week, suggesting continued decline toward resolution
CP is exactly at the threshold (11.00%) - needs ANY upward movement to resolve YES, but status quo drift likely pushes it below
Active high-level diplomatic talks (Pakistan quartet: Turkey, Egypt, Saudi Arabia) specifically focused on reopening Hormuz - could produce positive headlines that shift CP
Trump says deal 'could come very soon' and extended deadline to April 6 - creates potential forcing function for diplomacy
Underlying situation is dire: only 3-7 ships/day vs 45 needed; 95% traffic drop; Iran hardening control with military authorization requirements
War escalating: Houthis joining, Iran proposing permanent toll system, no signs of actual de-escalation on ground
Iran's ceasefire demands are extreme (full US withdrawal, sanctions lifted, compensation) - makes quick deal very unlikely
10 days is enough time for significant diplomatic developments; active war situations can shift quickly

reasoning:
**Forecast: 33% probability** that the CP will be above 11% on April 9.

**Key reasoning:**

- **CP trend is strongly bearish**: Dropped from 20% → 11% in one week. With 110 forecasters and the situation worsening, the natural trajectory is continued decline.
- **The threshold is razor-thin**: CP is at exactly 11.00%, meaning it needs *any* upward movement. But the momentum is clearly downward.
- **Ground truth is terrible**: Only 3-7 ships/day transit (vs. 45 needed). Iran is formalizing permanent control over the strait. Houthis have joined the war. Traffic is down 95%.
- **Diplomatic wildcard**: Active talks in Pakistan (Turkey/Egypt/Saudi Arabia quartet) and Trump's April 6 deadline create a ~20% chance of meaningful positive developments that could spike the CP.
- **But deals take time**: Iran's ceasefire demands are extreme, and even a ceasefire wouldn't instantly restore 45+ ships/day of traffic.

The most likely outcome (~65%) is the CP continues drifting down to 5-9% by April 9 as the situation remains grim. But there's a meaningful ~33% chance that diplomatic progress or positive headlines push it above the threshold.

resolution_criteria: This question will resolve based on the community prediction of the Metaculus question found [here](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42729) (i.e. the 'target question'). If the community prediction of the target question on 2026-04-09 03:46:10 is higher than 11.00%, this question will resolve to 'Yes'. If it is lower or equal to 11.00%, this question will resolve to 'No'. If the target question has already resolved before this question opens, then this question will be annulled. If the target question resolves after this question opens, but before 2026-04-09 03:46:10, then this question will resolve to the same value as the target question.
